friction_items.description: 344 tools, ~197 handlers in server.py (6813 lines), ~102 tool_audit_* hits. A fresh MCP client lists tools and has no triage path: there is no 'starter shortlist' of 15-20 core tools surfaced by default. For a theory project the AI needs maybe 6 tools (sys_boot, tool_route, sys_protocol_get, tool_step_complete, tool_path_finalize, tool_synthesize) but must reason past hundreds of empirical-flavoured tools (tool_audit_figure_*, tool_audit_evalue, tool_audit_statistical_power, tool_dashboard_*, tool_synthesis_curate_figures) that are categorically irrelevant for a proof. sys_active_tools narrows the surface — but the AI cannot call it until it has already routed to a protocol, so the bloat hits turn 1.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Phase 9 consolidation: gate empirical-statistical tools behind detected_pack ∈ {biology, engineering, qualitative}; surface ONLY theory-relevant tools (proof, lean, coq, dep_graph, lemma_library, search, redteam_review, synth) when detected_pack == theory_math. Or have sys_boot return a 15-tool shortlist scoped to the detected pack + a one-liner pointer to 'call sys_active_tools for the full surface.'

friction_items.description: tool_audit_step_literature description in server.py:1139 says 'every claim has a Verdict line (AGREES|DISAGREES|EXTENDS|DEFERRED)' — 4 verdicts. But literature_per_step.yaml lists SIX verdicts: AGREES, DISAGREES, EXTENDS, IMPORTED_AS_CITED, SPECIALIZES, DEFERRED. IMPORTED_AS_CITED is THE correct verdict for the theory case (citing Fulkerson-Gross 1965 for PEO existence as a lemma) — but a naive AI reading the tool description will not know it exists. The AI will use DEFERRED (wrong) or AGREES (semantically wrong, claim is not being independently verified), the gate will pass, the synthesis will read 'AGREES with Fulkerson-Gross' and the auditor or reviewer will flag it.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Sync tool_audit_step_literature.description with literature_per_step.yaml's full verdict enum: 'AGREES|DISAGREES|EXTENDS|IMPORTED_AS_CITED|SPECIALIZES|DEFERRED'. Add a one-line gloss for each in the tool description so the AI does not need to load the full protocol YAML to pick the right verdict.

friction_items.description: proof_verification_workflow step 1 (declare_step_contract) tells the AI to write step_summary.yaml with step_intent: proof + figure_required: false + literature_required: true with verdict IMPORTED_AS_CITED. This is the critical waiver that prevents tool_audit_step_completeness from blocking on missing figures. The waiver only works if the AI (a) reads proof_verification_workflow before any audit fires, (b) understands the YAML template enough to fill it correctly. Nothing at the tool surface (sys_active_tools, tool_audit_step_completeness description) tells the AI that step_intent: proof exists. A naive AI that calls tool_audit_step_completeness directly on a proof step (skipping the workflow's step 1) will be blocked with an opaque error about missing figures.
friction_items.suggested_fix: tool_audit_step_completeness should emit a helpful blocker message when figure_required is missing AND the step lives under workspace/proofs/ — 'this looks like a proof step; add step_intent: proof to step_summary.yaml to auto-waive the figure gate.' Or auto-detect proof intent from workspace/proofs/<slug>.md presence + a `## Claim` heading and infer the waiver.

friction_items.description: There is no obvious intake/onboarding protocol for 'I want to prove this conjecture, no data, no experiments.' The router likely picks guidance/project_startup which assumes a data project (asks about data location, target sample size, etc.). The theory_math pack has no intake protocol — proof_verification_workflow starts at 'claim already stated' and conjecture_tracking is a registry not an intake. A naive AI walks into the wizard, sees inputs/raw_data/ + inputs/literature/ scaffolded, and has nowhere to put the conjecture statement or the definitions of 'chordal graph' and 'treewidth'.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add theory_math/intake/theory_project_intake.yaml that (a) asks the right questions (what is the conjecture / venue / formal-tool intent / required prior results), (b) seeds inputs/preliminaries.md (which proof_strategy_selection's prereq references), (c) skips data-scaffolding prompts, (d) hands off to conjecture_tracking → proof_strategy_selection → proof_verification_workflow. Without this, the AI either guesses wrong (uses project_startup, fights its assumptions) or skips intake entirely and produces an inputs/ directory full of empty subdirs.

friction_items.description: tool_theory_math_lean_check / tool_theory_math_coq_check return status='warning' with a hint file when the binary is missing. The envelope is success-shaped ({status: success, data: {status: warning, hint_path, message}}). A naive AI parsing for status=='success' at the outer envelope will treat this as a successful proof check and proceed to publish a 'verified' proof that was never actually verified. The double-status nesting is a footgun: the outer status is the protocol-call status, the inner data.status is the lean-check status.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Make the install-missing case return status='warning' at the OUTER envelope, not nested. Or rename the inner field (data.lean_status / data.verification_status) so it cannot be confused with the envelope status. Plus: have the tool emit a clear 'PROOF NOT VERIFIED — Lean not installed' marker that downstream synthesis tools detect and refuse to claim formal verification.

friction_items.description: Two registries for the same idea: (a) state-level mem_hypothesis_add/update/list (empirical: status ∈ active|supported|refuted|pending), (b) workspace-level theory_math/conjecture/conjecture_tracking (math: status ∈ open|partial_progress|proven|disproven|counter_example_found). A naive AI sees both, has no doc explaining the relationship, and either maintains both (twice the work, two sources of truth that drift) or picks the wrong one (mem_hypothesis 'supported' is semantically wrong for a math conjecture — proof is binary).
friction_items.suggested_fix: Either (a) extend mem_hypothesis_* to accept project_kind='theory' and switch the status enum accordingly, then have conjecture_tracking write to mem_hypothesis under the hood, or (b) explicitly document in the conjecture_tracking trigger description that 'this REPLACES mem_hypothesis for theory projects — do not call both.' Right now both look applicable.

friction_items.description: tool_audit_step_completeness expects figures + tables for analytic steps. A proof step's natural output is workspace/proofs/<slug>.md (prose + equations) plus optionally a dependency graph mermaid file. The figure gate is supposed to auto-waive when step_intent: proof, but the waiver requires step_summary.yaml to be correctly populated FIRST. The naive AI's failure mode is: write the proof, call tool_step_complete (which calls audit_step_completeness), get blocked on 'no figures', not know that the waiver requires step_intent: proof in step_summary.yaml. Error message is opaque about how to waive.
friction_items.suggested_fix: When the figure gate fails AND step_intent is missing from step_summary.yaml, the blocker message should explicitly say 'add step_intent: proof|plan|ground|apparatus to step_summary.yaml to declare a non-empirical step and auto-waive figure/table requirements; see templates/step_summary.yaml.template'. Right now the AI sees the failure and may write a fake figure to satisfy the gate.

friction_items.description: If the AI phrases the prompt as 'I want to study chromatic numbers of chordal graphs' (no 'prove', no 'proof'), router will likely pick methodology/methodological_consultation or guidance/scope_clarification, both empirical-flavoured. The theory_math router_entries triggers (proof_strategy_selection has 'maximum degree', 'graph coloring', 'planar graph') are narrow — they will not catch 'chromatic number' or 'chordal' or 'treewidth'. A naive AI will not know to re-phrase using a trigger word.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Broaden theory_math triggers: add 'chromatic', 'colouring', 'chordal', 'treewidth', 'graph theory conjecture', 'extremal', 'spectral', 'algebraic' to either proof_strategy_selection or proof_verification_workflow router_entries. Or have tool_route, when confidence is medium and detector returns pack='theory_math' confidence > 0.4, suggest a theory_math protocol explicitly even if no trigger matches verbatim.

friction_items.description: conjecture_tracking.yaml has next_protocol: null. From a naive AI's POV, conjecture_tracking is registration → 'what now?' The natural next step is proof_strategy_selection → proof_verification_workflow but no chain points to it. The AI either calls protocol_completion and exits (premature), or has to read the pack docs to find the next step.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Set next_protocol: theory_math/method/proof_strategy_selection on conjecture_tracking when status is open OR partial_progress (the cases where work continues). Keep null for status=proven|disproven (terminal). Or document the typical theory_math pipeline (intake → conjecture_tracking → strategy_selection → proof_verification → theorem_dependency_graph → theory_paper_structure) in the pack-level docstring and surface it from tool_route's response.

friction_items.description: research_os_theory_math.__init__ defines paper_sections=(introduction, preliminaries, main_theorems, proofs, discussion). This is correct (non-IMRAD) and the synthesis pipeline reads it. But a naive AI calling tool_synthesize sees no signal that the section order will differ from IMRAD; the synthesis tool description doesn't mention pack-aware section ordering. AI cannot verify ahead of time that the right structure will emerge.
friction_items.suggested_fix: tool_synthesize description should say 'paper section order auto-adapts to detected pack (theory_math → preliminaries/main_theorems/proofs; biology → IMRAD; humanities → claim/evidence/interpretation)'. Add a dry-run mode that returns the planned section order without writing files.

friction_items.description: proof_verification_workflow step 5 (independent_review) tells the AI to call tool_redteam_review focus='proof'. The tool exists in core (not the theory_math pack) but the AI listing pack tools sees only the 3 theory_math tools (lean_check, coq_check, dep_graph). The cross-pack reference is correct but undiscoverable unless the AI already knows the tool exists.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Either (a) re-export tool_redteam_review in research_os_theory_math.tools so it appears in pack listings, or (b) add a 'related_tools: [tool_redteam_review, tool_search, mem_log]' field to PackRegistration that pack listings surface.

friction_items.description: tool_theory_math_dep_graph regex-parses imports + theorem names from .lean / .v files. The protocol's editorial_voice acknowledges this is a conservative lower bound — informal-prose citations are missed. A naive AI may treat the generated graph as ground truth and not run the manual_annotation step. The 20% manual_annotation_floor_pct is in the protocol YAML but not enforced by the tool.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Have tool_theory_math_dep_graph emit a 'coverage warning' in its return when n_imports / n_theorems < some threshold, prompting the AI to add manual annotations. Or have a companion tool tool_theory_math_dep_graph_audit that compares the auto-extracted graph against prose-citation candidates (regex over .md files for theorem/lemma name references) and emits a candidate-edges list for the AI to confirm or reject.

friction_items.description: researcher_config.yaml's writing_preferences.citation_style enum is apa|vancouver|acm|ieee|nature — none are the conventional math styles (amsplain, alpha, siam). A naive AI cannot configure the project for math venues and will end up with author-year APA citations in a paper destined for Annals of Mathematics (numerical alpha style).
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add amsplain, alpha, siam to citation_style enum; conditionally surface them when detected_pack == theory_math. Or accept arbitrary BibLaTeX style names and validate against the installed style files.

recommendations: Phase 9 consolidation: collapse the audit_* family behind a single tool_audit_run(dimension=...) dispatcher; gate empirical tools behind detected_pack to prevent surface bloat for theory projects.
Add theory_math/intake/theory_project_intake.yaml to bridge wizard → conjecture_tracking → strategy_selection; seed inputs/preliminaries.md instead of inputs/raw_data/ for theory projects.
Sync tool_audit_step_literature.description in server.py with the full 6-verdict enum in literature_per_step.yaml (add IMPORTED_AS_CITED + SPECIALIZES with one-line glosses).
Make the figure-gate blocker emit a 'add step_intent: proof to step_summary.yaml' hint when the step lives under workspace/proofs/, so the auto-waiver becomes discoverable from the error message.

Fix tool_theory_math_lean_check / tool_theory_math_coq_check so the install-missing case returns status='warning' at the OUTER envelope; right now the nested success/warning shape lets unverified proofs pass downstream gates.

Document (in tool_route output and pack-level docstring) the canonical theory_math pipeline: intake → conjecture_tracking → proof_strategy_selection → proof_verification_workflow → theorem_dependency_graph → theory_paper_structure.
Resolve the mem_hypothesis_* vs conjecture_tracking duplication: either merge or explicitly call out 'use conjecture_tracking for theory, mem_hypothesis for empirical' in both tool descriptions.

Broaden theory_math router triggers to include common conjecture-domain vocabulary (chromatic, chordal, treewidth, extremal, spectral, algebraic) so prompts without the literal word 'prove' still route correctly.
Add citation_style values amsplain|alpha|siam to researcher_config and surface them when detected_pack=='theory_math'.

Re-export tool_redteam_review and tool_search from research_os_theory_math.tools (or add a related_tools field to PackRegistration) so cross-pack tool references in protocols are discoverable from pack listings.
